relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks a sign meant to settle the priestly dispute.
Each tribal house brings one named staff.
Aaron's name is written on Levi's staff.
All the staffs rest together before the testimony.
The chosen staff will bud, and the murmuring will be answered.
Every tribe places its leadership sign before YHWH.
Moses leaves the staffs overnight in the tent.
Dead wood becomes bud, blossom, and ripe almond in one night.
Every leader receives his staff back after witnessing the sign.
Aaron's staff remains before the testimony as remembered boundary.
Moses keeps the sign as commanded.
The people answer not with peace but with terror of perishing.
Their fear asks whether sacred nearness has become impossible.

contemplative_note: Numbers 17 gives life-from-dead-wood as a sign of vocation, but the people's final terror prevents easy triumphalism. Legitimate service must still become understandable, accountable, and safe for the community.
